dates.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0, alongside the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview model and a Full-Duplex Speech LLM aimed at more natural, interruption-tolerant voice interaction. On the tooling side, yetone/native-feel-skill emerged as a notable agent-development artifact, packaging Raycast-inspired cross-platform desktop design patterns into a reusable skillset. Early research signals also pointed to model-quality tradeoffs and open-model momentum, with coverage citing GPT-5.5 performance gains alongside hallucination concerns and Kimi K2.6 leading open-LLM comparisons.
